title: Roegneria uralensis
intro: Roegneria uralensis, also known as Ural wheatgrass or Roegneria, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been reported to aid in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Roegneria uralensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Roegneria uralensis is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Roegneria uralensis is a species of grass in the genus Roegneria. It belongs to the family Poaceae. The genus Roegneria is part of the subfamily Pooideae, which includes various grass species. The family Poaceae, also known as the grass family, is one of the largest families of flowering plants. The classification of Roegneria uralensis reflects its position within the botanical hierarchy.
